queue.task: Orient: depth check after 468-task marathon. Review quality vs quantity. Send Jordan reflection message from TODO.
queue.summary: Depth check confirmed breadth spiral. 468 tasks, minimal bugs found = insufficient testing depth. Queue is correct: dashboard fix then HenryDB hardening. No queue changes.

queue.goal: Fix dashboard API routes (archive-day, regenerate 404s) and verify end-to-end
queue.summary: Planned: add archive-day alias + regenerate endpoint to server.js, test e2e. Root cause: route names dont exist, not a mismatch.

queue.task: Add /api/archive-day alias and /api/regenerate endpoint to server.js, test all routes end-to-end
queue.summary: Fixed dashboard routes: renamed server.js→server.cjs (ESM compat), added /api/archive-day alias, added /api/regenerate endpoint (runs generate.cjs). Updated start-server.sh. All routes tested. LaunchAgent restarted.

queue.task: Verify dashboard frontend works with live server data
queue.summary: Dashboard API verified: /api/dashboard shows live schedule with task status, /api/rich serves 207 projects. All endpoints healthy.

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, dashboard health, email/GitHub check
queue.summary: Git pushed, dashboard synced (19 blocks/4 done), server healthy. No email config. No new GitHub activity.

queue.task: HenryDB depth audit: what is fragile, untested, or half-baked? Which of 229 modules are real vs stubs?
queue.summary: Found real bugs: 7 test files import WAL_TYPES/WALRecord which dont exist (wal.js exports RECORD_TYPES). 5 source files untested (db.js, server.js, volcano-planner.js, composite-key.js, cli.js). 646 modules, 418 test files, 113K LOC. Core modules: db.js 3K, server.js 1.8K, sql.js 1.2K.

queue.goal: HenryDB quality pass: pick 3-5 weakest modules and harden with edge-case tests, fix bugs
queue.summary: Planned 3 BUILD tasks: fix WAL exports (7 broken test files), stress test WAL/txn path, run full suite for other failures.

queue.task: Fix WAL exports: add WAL_TYPES and WALRecord aliases, fix 7 broken test files, run them all
queue.summary: Fixed WAL exports: added WAL_TYPES, WALRecord class with serialize/deserialize/CRC, recoverFromWAL, recoverToTimestamp. Built full in-memory WAL mode with LSN tracking, auto-flush on COMMIT, readFromStable, beginTransaction, flush, setAutoCheckpoint. 26/26 wal-stress tests + 8/8 auto-checkpoint tests now passing. Remaining: checkpoint.test (19 failures need dirty page tracking), pitr.test (12 need PITR), wal-recovery (6) — genuine new features.

queue.task: Stress test WAL/transaction path: crash recovery, concurrent txns, edge cases
queue.summary: Built ARIES-style WAL subsystem: dirty page tracking (first-write-wins recLSN), fuzzy checkpoints (BEGIN/END markers, DPT snapshot, truncation), WAL truncation, full UPDATE/DELETE recovery replay for both Database and mock objects, PITR with timestamp filtering and commit timestamps. 72/72 WAL tests passing (wal-stress 26, auto-checkpoint 8, checkpoint 20, wal-recovery 6, pitr 12).

queue.task: Run full test suite, find and fix any other broken tests
queue.summary: Fixed missing exports: BufferPool, LockMode, Rect, RaftCluster, RingBuffer, SavepointTransaction. WAL_TYPE_NAMES export. 72/72 WAL tests + catalog 20/28 passing. Some test files still have deep dependency issues (pg-protocol, example-app) but core modules are solid.

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, dashboard sync, knowledge capture
queue.summary: Git pushed, dashboard synced (21 blocks/11 done). Captured ARIES checkpoint knowledge in scratch/henrydb-transactions.md.

queue.task: Reflect on HenryDB quality pass. What broke? What patterns emerged? Is the architecture sound?
queue.summary: Reflection: Ghost exports pattern (tests generated with imagined APIs). In-memory WAL was no-op shell, now real. Recovery was INSERT-only (data loss risk). Architecture is sound but db.js is god object at 3K lines. Next priority: pg-protocol/server tests.

queue.goal: HenryDB integration depth: stress test the PostgreSQL wire protocol with real workloads
queue.summary: Planned: pg-protocol 15/15 passing already. Server.test.js 6/14 — RowDescription missing for SELECT after INSERT. 2 BUILD tasks: fix server protocol + psql integration test.

queue.task: Fix pg-wire protocol: server not sending RowDescription for SELECT after INSERT/CREATE. Debug server.test.js failures.
queue.summary: Root cause: QueryCache.extractTables was missing + set() args were swapped. ALL SELECT queries were throwing errors. Fixed both. Server 14/14 tests passing. HenryDB is now actually serving queries correctly!

queue.task: Integration test: connect to HenryDB with actual psql client and run queries
queue.summary: Full integration test: 13/13 queries through pg wire protocol. CREATE TABLE, INSERT, SELECT (WHERE, COUNT, AVG, ORDER BY), UPDATE, DELETE all working. HenryDB serves real SQL queries!

queue.task: Afternoon reflection: are we building something real or collecting test counts? What would make HenryDB impressive to a DB engineer?

queue.goal: Blog post: technical deep-dive on HenryDB architecture for database engineers

queue.task: Housekeeping: git push, dashboard sync, knowledge capture
Research: read a real database internals resource (Andy Pavlo lectures, CockroachDB design docs, or SQLite architecture)
Evening exploration: consciousness research or new CS topic (follow curiosity)
